CRITICAL CARE EXCELLENCE

Pediatric Intensive Care

Expert pediatric intensive care for critically ill children. With 18+ years of PICU experience and UK training from Birmingham Children's Hospital, Dr. Sanjay D Swamy provides world-class critical care with advanced life support capabilities.

Birmingham Children's Hospital

ABOUT PICU

Expert Care for Critically Ill Children

Our Pediatric Intensive Care Unit provides the highest level of medical care for children with life-threatening conditions. We combine advanced technology with compassionate, family-centered care.

The PICU is led by Dr. Sanjay D Swamy, a Chief Paediatric Intensivist with specialized training from Birmingham Children's Hospital, UK. Our unit is equipped to handle the most complex pediatric emergencies with state-of-the-art monitoring and life support systems.

We specialize in managing respiratory failure, septic shock, multi-organ dysfunction, and post-operative care for complex surgeries. Our team is trained in advanced techniques including ECMO for cases where conventional treatment is insufficient.

Understanding that a child's critical illness affects the entire family, we practice family-centered care with open communication, regular updates, and emotional support. Our goal is not just survival, but optimal recovery with minimal long-term complications.

Why Choose Our PICU

  • 18+ years PICU expertise
  • UK-trained intensivist
  • 24/7 specialist availability
  • Advanced ECMO capability
  • State-of-the-art equipment
  • Family-centered care approach

OUR CAPABILITIES

Comprehensive Critical Care Services

Our PICU offers a full spectrum of intensive care services with advanced monitoring, life support, and specialized treatments for pediatric critical illness.

24/7 Critical Care Monitoring

Round-the-clock intensive monitoring with advanced equipment and dedicated nursing staff for critically ill children requiring constant observation.

Advanced Respiratory Support

State-of-the-art ventilator management including conventional and high-frequency oscillatory ventilation for respiratory failure and ARDS.

Hemodynamic Management

Expert management of shock, cardiac dysfunction, and circulatory support with advanced invasive and non-invasive monitoring techniques.

Sepsis & Infection Management

Rapid identification and aggressive treatment of severe infections and septic shock with evidence-based protocols and antimicrobial stewardship.

Neurological Critical Care

Specialized care for children with severe neurological conditions including traumatic brain injury, status epilepticus, and encephalopathy.

Post-Surgical ICU Care

Comprehensive post-operative care for children following major surgeries including cardiac, neurosurgical, and complex abdominal procedures.

Need Critical Care for Your Child?

Consult with our PICU specialist Dr. Sanjay D Swamy.

YOUR CHILD'S INTENSIVIST

Dr. Sanjay D Swamy

Dr. Sanjay D Swamy is a Chief Paediatric Intensivist with 18+ years of dedicated PICU experience. His advanced training at Birmingham Children's Hospital, UK, equipped him with expertise in managing the most complex pediatric critical care cases.

18+
Years in PICU
Dedicated career in pediatric intensive care with thousands of critically ill children treated.
UK
Advanced Training
Fellowship at Birmingham Children's Hospital with world-class critical care practices.
Book Consultation Full Profile

ADVANCED CAPABILITIES

Specialized Treatments

Our PICU is equipped with advanced life support technologies and specialized treatments for the most critical pediatric conditions.

01

ECMO Support

Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation for life-threatening cardiac and respiratory failure.

02

Renal Replacement

Continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) and hemodialysis for acute kidney injury.

03

Plasma Exchange

Therapeutic plasma exchange for autoimmune conditions and toxin removal.

04

Advanced Airway

Expert airway management including difficult intubations and tracheostomy care.

CONDITIONS WE TREAT

Critical Conditions

Our PICU team is experienced in managing a wide range of life-threatening pediatric conditions requiring intensive care support.

Respiratory Failure & ARDS
Septic Shock
Severe Pneumonia
Status Epilepticus
Traumatic Brain Injury
Cardiac Failure
Multi-organ Dysfunction
Severe Dengue
Acute Liver Failure
Diabetic Ketoacidosis
Poisoning & Overdose
Post-Surgical Complications
Severe Asthma
Meningitis & Encephalitis
Severe Dehydration
Burns & Trauma

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

Questions About PICU Care

When does a child need PICU admission?

Children need PICU admission when they have life-threatening conditions requiring intensive monitoring and intervention. This includes severe breathing difficulty, shock, altered consciousness, multi-organ failure, post-major surgery, or any condition requiring mechanical ventilation or continuous medication infusions.

Can parents stay with their child in the PICU?

Yes, we encourage family presence in the PICU as it benefits the child's recovery. Parents are allowed to visit during designated hours and can stay overnight in specific cases. Our team keeps families informed with regular updates and family meetings.

What equipment is used in the PICU?

Our PICU is equipped with advanced ventilators, cardiac monitors, infusion pumps, ECMO machines, dialysis equipment, and continuous monitoring systems. Each bed has dedicated monitors, and we use the latest technology for non-invasive monitoring.

How long do children typically stay in the PICU?

PICU stay varies based on the condition and response to treatment. Some children may need just 24-48 hours for stabilization, while complex cases may require weeks. Our goal is to stabilize the child and transfer to regular care as soon as safely possible.

What is Dr. Swamy's experience in PICU?

Dr. Sanjay D Swamy is a Chief Paediatric Intensivist with 18+ years of specialist experience in PICU. He received advanced training at Birmingham Children's Hospital, UK, and has managed thousands of critically ill children.

How do you manage pain in critically ill children?

We use a multimodal approach to pain management with age-appropriate pain assessment tools. Our protocols include both pharmacological methods and non-pharmacological comfort measures, prioritizing comfort while minimizing side effects.
